title: Parkio Daily for May 13, 2026
teaser: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster transforms with The Muppets next week. Beach Club work extends further, and Halloween party dates selling fast.

rides.name: Remy's Ratatouille Adventure
rides.note: Mid-week mornings usually yield sub-20 minute waits

rides.name: Tiana's Bayou Adventure
rides.note: First hour entry tends to keep lines manageable

rides.name: Slinky Dog Dash
rides.note: Rope drop window often best for this one

breaking.title: Rock 'n' Roller Coaster reopens May 26 with The Muppets theme
breaking.body: Hollywood Studios' reimagined coaster drops guests into The Electric Mayhem's biggest concert night. Five-track set list features the band plus friends, new psychedelic guitar marquee out front, and G-Force Records queue theming throughout.

parkioInsight.text: Sunset Boulevard tends to see heavy foot traffic on reopening days. Riding during evening hours or using Lightning Lane may help avoid multi-hour standby waits in the first week.

bignews.title: Beach Club refurb timeline pushed back again
bignews.body: Disney's Beach Club Resort construction now has a revised end date. The resort has seen multiple timeline adjustments as work continues.

parkioInsight.text: Resort construction noise and closed amenities can affect your stay quality. Consider nearby Yacht Club or BoardWalk for quieter EPCOT-area lodging until work wraps.

bignews.title: Halloween party's priciest night sold out in hours
bignews.body: The $229 Mickey's Not So Scary date sold faster than any previous year. Other peak dates likely to follow as general ticket sales opened this week.

parkioInsight.text: Early September and late October weeknights usually have better availability and lower per-ticket costs. Booking those dates now often saves money and stress.

topstories.title: Gideon's Bakehouse adds new cake slice this month
topstories.body: The popular Disney Springs bakery debuted a fresh cake slice flavor. Lines typically form early, especially on weekends.

parkioInsight.text: Gideon's new items often sell out by early afternoon. Visiting Disney Springs before noon usually improves your chances of snagging the latest treat.

topstories.title: Haunted Mansion Loungefly backpack now up for pre-order
topstories.body: BoxLunch exclusive mini backpack features the Disneyland mansion facade with Hitchhiking Ghosts and opening Stretching Room portrait panels. Priced at $84.90.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition Loungefly releases tend to sell out quickly online and at park retail locations. Pre-ordering now likely secures the item before in-park stock disappears.

topstories.title: Walt Disney World president announces retirement
topstories.body: Jeff Vahle's departure follows recent leadership reshuffling across Disney Parks and Resorts. No successor named yet.

parkioInsight.text: Leadership transitions rarely affect day-to-day park operations. Guests visiting in the next few months should see no immediate service or policy changes.

icymi.title: Disneyland updates pin trading rules in Frontierland
icymi.body: New etiquette guidelines eliminate designated trading spots. Handouts being distributed to pin collectors at the California park.

icymi.title: Tokyo Disneyland's Space Mountain Earthrise uses spinning vehicles
icymi.body: Construction photos reveal ride system similar to Cosmic Rewind but not identical. The project is shaping up to be one of Imagineering's costliest attractions ever.

spotlight.title: Disney Springs exploration pays off mid-week
spotlight.body: With Gideon's new offerings, updated Planet Hollywood menu items, and lighter weekday foot traffic, the shopping and dining district offers solid variety away from park crowds. Late morning through early afternoon tends to be the sweet spot for browsing without congestion.

parkioInsight.text: Weekday afternoons at Disney Springs usually mean shorter waits at popular restaurants and easier parking. Arriving around 11am often beats both lunch rushes and evening dinner crowds.
